text.title: Go Backend Development Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every Go Backend Development Patterns application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Layout MUST follow `cmd/<bin>` + `internal/<feature>` + `pkg/<lib>`; no other top-level dirs without ADR.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go community layout (golang-standards repo + critiques). Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Handler imports service interface; service imports repository interface; repository never imports up.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Hexagonal architecture. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Every public function that crosses a layer or does I/O takes `context.Context` as first arg.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go blog + context docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Errors crossing layer boundaries MUST be `*apperror.AppError` (wrapped with `%w`), never bare `errors.New`.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: go-error-handling methodology. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: main() MUST listen for SIGINT/SIGTERM, `Shutdown(ctx)` HTTP server, wait on `WaitGroup`, then exit.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: stdlib net/http docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Interfaces MUST be defined where consumed (handler defines `UserService`), never at the producer.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: Go proverb 'accept interfaces, return structs'. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

rule.statement: Background goroutines MUST be scoped to a `context.Context` and tracked in a `WaitGroup`.
rule.rationale: Rule rooted in source: go-goroutines + goleak docs. Violations trigger the matching antipattern in 03-failure-modes.xml.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold and no duplicate exists.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Update the existing artefact in place (bump version, refresh last_reviewed).
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when a duplicate is found.
